sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
[Collections](manual/reference/glossary) are groupings of MongoDB documents,
similar to an RDBMS (Relational Database Management System)
table. A collection exists within a single database.

Even if a collection doesn't contain any documents, it still comes with
a resource cost because you can't drop the [default \_id index](manual/indexes). For small collections, this index doesn't
take up much space. If you have thousands of collections, these indexes
can add up in resources and strain your database allocation.

If your deployment contains unnecessary or a large number of collections
that keeps increasing, to reduce the resource requirements of your application,
restructure your data to remove unnecessary collections.

sections.heading: Example
sections.body:
Consider a `temperatures` database that stores collections of
temperature readings obtained from a sensor. The sensor takes readings
every half hour from 10 AM to 10 PM. Each day's readings are stored in a
separate collection, named by the reading date:

```javascript
// temperatures.march-09-2020

{
  "_id": 1,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-09T010:00:00Z",
  "temperature": 29
}
{
  "_id": 2,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-09T010:30:00Z",
  "temperature": 30
}
...
{
  "_id": 25,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-09T022:00:00Z",
  "temperature": 26
}
```

```javascript
// temperatures.march-10-2020

{
  "_id": 1,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-10T010:00:00Z",
  "temperature": 30
}
{
  "_id": 2,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-10T010:30:00Z",
  "temperature": 32
}
...
{
  "_id": 25,
  "timestamp": "2020-03-10T022:00:00Z",
  "temperature": 28
}
```

With each passing day, the number of collections in the database
increments. Since the number of collections is unbounded, there is an
ever-growing need from the database to maintain these collections and
their corresponding indexes. If the database eventually reaches a point
where it is managing thousands of collections and indexes, it may result
in performance degradation.

Additionally, this approach does not easily facilitate queries across
multiple days. To query data from multiple days to obtain temperature
trends over longer periods of time, you would need to perform a
[$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup) operation, which is not as performant as querying
data in the same collection.

sections.heading: Updated Schema
How to Check for Unnecessary Collections
MongoDB Shell
sections.body:
To check the number of collections in your database, you can
run the following command from [mongosh](mongodb-shell):

```javascript
db.getCollectionNames().length
```

The [db.stats()](manual/reference/method/db.stats) method also
returns the number of collections in your database, along with useful
database statistics such as the total size of your data and indexes.

sections.heading: Atlas UI
sections.body:
If the [Atlas UI](atlas-ui) is enabled, the Atlas UI
provides a high-level overview of collections in your databases. The
Atlas UI shows the total size of a collection, including the size of
a collection's indexes. If the majority of a collection's size is
comprised of indexes, you can consider consolidating that collection's
data into another collection and dropping the original collection. Refer
to the [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) documentation for an approach to merging data
from one collection into another.

Additionally, if the Atlas UI reveals that you have empty
collections, you can drop those collections directly from the
Atlas UI.

sections.heading: Real-Time Performance Panel
sections.body:
The Atlas [Real-Time Performance Panel](real-time-metrics-status-tab) shows which collections receive the most
activity. You can use this tool to ensure that before you drop a
collection, it is not being actively used by your application.

sections.heading: Learn More
sections.body:
- To learn more about Data Modeling in MongoDB and the flexible schema
  model, see [Data Modeling Introduction](manual/core/data-modeling-introduction).

- To learn more about databases and collections, see
  [Databases and Collections](manual/core/databases-and-collections).

- To learn more about embedding related data in a single document, see
  [Embedded Data Models](manual/data-modeling)

sections.heading: MongoDB.live 2020 Presentations
sections.body:
To learn how to incorporate the flexible data model into your schema,
see the following presentations from **MongoDB.live 2020**:

- Learn about entity relationships in MongoDB and examples of their
  implementations with
  [Data Modeling with MongoDB](https://www.mongodb.com/presentations/data-modeling-with-mongodb).

- Learn advanced data modeling design patterns you can incorporate
  into your schema with
  [Advanced Schema Design Patterns](https://www.mongodb.com/presentations/advanced-schema-design-patterns).
